data.title: As a Jira user, I can see a ticket's file impact in Jira as both an issue property and a formatted comment
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Extend the Jira outbound bridge to surface file impact via (a) a Jira issue property (key: dso.file_impact) set via direct REST PUT to /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/properties/dso.file_impact using a new AcliClient._direct_rest_put() method; (b) a single formatted Jira comment deduped by UUID marker via the existing .jira-comment-map dedup mechanism.

**Why**: Machine-readable storage in Jira issue property + human visibility via Jira comment. Enables preplanning agents and reviewers consuming Jira to see structured file impact data.

**Scope**:
- IN: AcliClient._direct_rest_put() method with credential header injection, outbound bridge code path for FILE_IMPACT events, formatted comment generation with UUID marker, dedup via existing .jira-comment-map, inbound bridge confirmation that dso.file_impact property is ignored (it lives outside fields.*), unit tests (mock PUT, assert credential header injection), integration test
- OUT: bridge-outbound.py FILE_IMPACT event routing and BRIDGE_ALERT emission (Story D); migration (Story E); consumer refactors (Story F)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the outbound bridge PUTs dso.file_impact to /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/properties/dso.file_impact with correct JSON body and Authorization header ← Satisfies: "Jira outbound bridge surfaces file impact in Jira using two mechanisms: (a) a Jira issue property (key: dso.file_impact, set via direct REST API PUT to /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/properties/dso.file_impact using AcliClient._direct_rest_put() — a new method justified by spike confirming ACLI has no properties subcommand) for machine-readable storage"
- When this story is complete, the outbound bridge posts a single formatted comment deduped by UUID marker via the existing .jira-comment-map ← Satisfies: "(b) a single formatted comment (deduped by UUID marker via the existing comment dedup mechanism and .jira-comment-map) for human visibility"
- When this story is complete, the inbound bridge does NOT read dso.file_impact as a ticket field (the property lives outside fields.*) and the outbound comment is deduplicated via the existing dedup map ← Satisfies: "The inbound bridge ignores the dso.file_impact property (properties live outside fields.*) and deduplicates the outbound comment via the existing dedup map"
- When this story is complete, unit tests mock the PUT and assert credential header injection ← Satisfies: "unit tests mocking the PUT and asserting credential header injection"
- When this story is complete, an integration test confirms the full outbound path (property PUT + comment) against a mocked Jira

## Considerations

- [Reliability] AcliClient already stores credentials on self — _direct_rest_put() consumes those credentials for the Authorization header
- [Reliability] Bridge auth: if API token, no scope change required; if OAuth 2.0, confirm write:jira-work scope is present before merging
- [Maintainability] _direct_rest_put() is the ACLI-only pattern break — document it in AcliClient with the spike reference
- [Testing] The RED integration test in story b9ac-2459 must be GREEN at the end of this story's implementation

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
